perturbation.original_text: 3. Payments. a. In consideration of the Sponsorship Benefits to be furnished by SDBF to Sponsor hereunder, Sponsor shall pay to SDBF the Annual Payments set forth in the Agreement Summary on the dates set forth therein. Any payment due pursuant to this Section that is not received by SDBF within fifteen (15) days after such payment is due shall bear interest from the date the payment was due until paid, at a per annum rate of interest equal to the prime rate of interest as published in the Wall Street Journal, plus five (5) percentage points, or if such interest rate is not permitted by applicable law, at the highest non-usurious interest rate permitted by applicable law. At its sole option, SDBF may terminate this Agreement in the event that a payment required hereunder is not made within fifteen (15) days of the date such payment is due and may exercise all other rights and remedies available to SDBF.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Payments. a. In consideration of the Sponsorship Benefits to be furnished by SDBF to Sponsor hereunder, Sponsor shall pay to SDBF the Annual Payments set forth in the Agreement Summary on the dates set forth therein. Any payment due pursuant to this Section that is not received by SDBF within thirty (30) days after such payment is due shall bear interest from the date the payment was due until paid, at a per annum rate of interest equal to the prime rate of interest as published in the Wall Street Journal, plus five (5) percentage points, or if such interest rate is not permitted by applicable law, at the highest non-usurious interest rate permitted by applicable law. At its sole option, SDBF may terminate this Agreement in the event that a payment required hereunder is not made within fifteen (15) days of the date such payment is due and may exercise all other rights and remedies available to SDBF.
perturbation.explanation: The payment term is now changed to thirty (30) days, which contradicts the provision in the same paragraph stating that SDBF may terminate the agreement if payment is not made within fifteen (15) days. This creates an in-text contradiction as to when SDBF has the right to terminate the agreement due to non-payment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Payments. a. In consideration of the Sponsorship Benefits to be furnished by SDBF to Sponsor hereunder, Sponsor shall pay to SDBF the Annual Payments set forth in the Agreement Summary on the dates set forth therein. Any payment due pursuant to this Section that is not received by SDBF within thirty (30) days after such payment is due shall bear interest from the date the payment was due until paid, at a per annum rate of interest equal to the prime rate of interest as published in the Wall Street Journal, plus five (5) percentage points, or if such interest rate is not permitted by applicable law, at the highest non-usurious interest rate permitted by applicable law. At its sole option, SDBF may terminate this Agreement in the event that a payment required hereunder is not made within fifteen (15) days of the date such payment is due and may exercise all other rights and remedies available to SDBF.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the payment term to thirty (30) days, directly contradicting the original provision allowing SDBF to terminate the agreement if payment is not made within fifteen (15) days. This creates a clear in-text contradiction, as the same paragraph now states two different timeframes for payment before SDBF can exercise its termination rights, leading to ambiguity and potential legal disputes. The contradicting sections are located within the same paragraph: 'Any payment due pursuant to this Section that is not received by SDBF within thirty (30) days after such payment is due shall bear interest...' and 'At its sole option, SDBF may terminate this Agreement in the event that a payment required hereunder is not made within fifteen (15) days of the date such payment is due...'
